title: Error: no FROM statement found when buildin rootless-cni-infra for RHEL
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

The instructions to build rootless-cni-infra (https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/v3.0.1-rhel/contrib/rootless-cni-infra ) on RHEL leads to `Error: no FROM statement found` with `podman version 3.0.2-dev` 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
[mgoerens@mgoerens dev]$ mkdir build_cni_image
[mgoerens@mgoerens dev]$ cd build_cni_image
[mgoerens@mgoerens build_cni_image]$ wget https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v3.0.1-rhel/contrib/rootless-cni-infra/Containerfile
[mgoerens@mgoerens build_cni_image]$ wget https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v3.0.1-rhel/contrib/rootless-cni-infra/rootless-cni-infra
[mgoerens@mgoerens build_cni_image]$ podman build -t rootless-cni-infra .
Error: no FROM statement found
[mgoerens@mgoerens build_cni_image]$ podman --version
podman version 3.0.2-dev
```

**Describe the results you received:**

`Error: no FROM statement found`

**Describe the results you expected:**

Image being built

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.0.2-dev
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
[mgoerens@mgoerens build_cni_image]$ podman info --debug

Size         : 46 M

From repo    : rhel-8-for-x86_64-appstream-rpms

URL          : https://podman.io/
License      : ASL 2.0 and GPLv3+
Description  : podman (Pod Manager) is a fully featured container engine that is a simple
             : daemonless tool.  podman provides a Docker-CLI comparable command line that
             : eases the transition from other container engines and allows the management of
             : pods, containers and images.  Simply put: alias docker=podman.
             : Most podman commands can be run as a regular user, without requiring
             : additional privileges.
             : 
             : podman uses Buildah(1) internally to create container images.
             : Both tools share image (not container) storage, hence each can use or
             : manipulate images (but not containers) created by the other.
             : 
             : Manage Pods, Containers and Container Images
             : podman Simple management tool for pods, containers and images

[mgoerens@mgoerens build_cni_image]$ rpm -q podman
podman-3.0.1-7.module+el8.4.0+11311+9da8acfb.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes, with the latest version apparently available to me, installed with:

```
sudo yum module enable -y container-tools:rhel8
sudo yum module install -y container-tools:rhel8
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: You cannot download from github links directly, you have to use the **raw** version, `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/containers/podman/v3.0.1-rhel/contrib/rootless-cni-infra/Containerfile` and `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/containers/podman/v3.0.1-rhel/contrib/rootless-cni-infra/rootless-cni-infra`. If you download from `github.com` you get html and not the actual  file content.
